content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission


July 15, 1988


Beverly Anglin, Filing Officer
Pleasanton Unified School District
4665 Bernal Avenue
Pleasanton, California 94566-7498

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-88-200

Dear Ms. Anglin:

You have requested advice concerning the conflict of interest disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1/

FACTS

The Amador/Pleasanton School District is being reorganized and will become the Pleasanton Unified School District. The Amador/Pleasanton School District's conflict of interest code will terminate as of July 1, 1988. Since it is no longer a multi-county agency, the Fair Political Practices Commission will no longer be the code reviewing body for this school district. (Section 82011(b).)


1/Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

Because you are seeking clarification of the filing obligations of persons who are not specifically identified, and who have not authorized you to seek advice on their behalf, this letter constitutes informal assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c).


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 207 • Sacramento CA 95804-0207 • (916) 322-5660

Beverly Anglin
Page Two
July 15, 1988

The jurisdiction of the new Pleasanton Unified School
District is wholly within Alameda County which is the new code
reviewing body. You advised me that Alameda County recently
adopted a conflict of interest code for this new school district.

QUESTIONS

1. What are the filing obligations for the eight board
members retiring from board service?

2. What are the filing obligations for the two board
members who will serve on the new Pleasanton Unified District?

CONCLUSIONS

1. The eight board members who are retiring from board
service as of July 1, 1988, will be required to file a leaving
office statement of economic interests (Form 730) within 30 days
of leaving office. The statements are due on August 1, 1988.2/

2. The two board members who will serve on the new
Pleasanton Unified School District Board must file leaving
office statements of economic interests (Form 730) within 30
days of the effective date of the district's conflict of
interest code. These statements are due by August 1, 1988.2/

The new conflict of interest code for the Pleasanton Unified
School District will become effective 30 days after the date of
approval. Each member sitting of the new district's board will
be required to file an initial statement within 30 days after
the effective date of the conflict of interest code.

ANALYSIS

The Act requires each state or local government agency to
adopt a conflict of interest code within six months after it
comes into existence. The conflict of interest code requires
designated employees to disclose certain investments, interests
in real property, business positions and income. (Section
87303.)

2/When the deadline for filing a statement falls on a
Saturday, Sunday or official state holiday, the deadline is
extended to the next regular business day. (Regulation 18116).
Since the filing deadline of July 30 falls on a Saturday, the
deadline is extended.

Beverly Anglin
Page Three
July 15, 1988

     Each designated employee must file an initial statement
within 30 days after the effective date of the conflict of
interest code. Designated employees also must file annual
statements. For agencies which have adopted the standard
conflict of interest code (Regulation 18730), annual statements
are due by April 1st each year. Upon leaving office each
designated employee must file a leaving office statement within
30 days. (Section 87303.)

     The Fair Political Practices Commission is the code reviewing
body for multi-county agencies. The board of supervisors is the
code reviewing body for any local government agency, other than
a city agency, with jurisdiction wholly within the county.
(Section 82011(b).)

     Please call me at (916) 322-5662 if you have additional
questions regarding this matter.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        Diane M. Griffiths
                                        General Counsel
                                        [illegible]
                                        Alice Hughes
                                        Technical Assistance and
                                          Analysis Division

Amador-Pleasanton Public Schools
4665 Bernal Avenue • Pleasanton, CA 94566-7498
(415) 462-5500

May 23, 1988


Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Gentlemen:

As of July 1, 1988, the Amador/Pleasanton School
Districts will become the Pleasanton Unified District.
There will be eight of the ten board members of these
two boards retiring from board service. Do they each
fill out a "Leaving Office Statement"? If so, would
you please send me 8 such statements. Also, would the
two board members who will serve on the new Pleasanton
Unified Board fill out "Initial Statements" for the
new board, as well as the three new members. If so,
please send me the necessary forms for this action.

I await your direction in this matter.

Sincerely,

Beverly Anglin

BEVERLY ANGLIN
Filing Officer for the
Amador/Pleasanton Districts

BA


BOARDS OF TRUSTEES

AMADOR
Donald C. Biddie, President
David C. Melander, Clerk
Gail Fairfield
Juanita Haugen
L.B. Perreira

PLEASANTON
Clark Guthrie, President
Earnestine Schneider, Clerk
Charles Edsinger
Nancy Hawley
Dr L. Bruce Merrill

SUPERINTENDENT
Dr Bill J. James

Amador-Pleasanton Public Schools
4665 Bernal Avenue • Pleasanton, CA 94566-7498
(415) 462-5500

May 23, 1988


Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Gentlemen:

As of July 1, 1988, the Amador/Pleasanton School
Districts will become the Pleasanton Unified District.
There will be eight of the ten board members of these
two boards retiring from board service. Do they each
fill out a "Leaving Office Statement"? If so, would
you please send me 8 such statements. Also, would the
two board members who will serve on the new Pleasanton
Unified Board fill out "Initial Statements" for the
new board, as well as the three new members. If so,
please send me the necessary forms for this action.

I await your direction in this matter.

Sincerely,

Beverly Anglin
BEVERLY ANGLIN
Filing Officer for the
Amador/Pleasanton Districts

BA


BOARDS OF TRUSTEES

AMADOR
Donald C. Biddie, President
David C. Melander, Clerk
Gail Fairfield
Juanita Haugen
L.B. Pereira


PLEASANTON
Clark Gunson, President
Earnestine Schneider, Clerk
Charles Eddinger
Nancy Hawtrey
Dr. L. Bruce Merrill


SUPERINTENDENT
Dr. Bill J. James

Amador Valley Joint Union High School District • Pleasanton Joint School District
